use anyhow::Result;
use thiserror::Error;

#[derive(Debug, Error)]
pub enum ApiError {
    #[error("upstream timeout")]
    Timeout,
    #[error("permission denied")]
    PermissionDenied,
    #[error("resource not found: {0}")]
    NotFound(String),
    #[error("rate limited")]
    RateLimited,
    #[error(transparent)]
    Other(#[from] anyhow::Error),
}

fn handle_api_error(result: Result<Response>) -> String {
    match result {
        Ok(resp) => format!("ok: {}", resp.body),
        Err(e) => match e.downcast_ref::<ApiError>() {
            Some(ApiError::Timeout) => "retry later".to_string(),
            Some(ApiError::PermissionDenied) => "unauthorized".to_string(),
            Some(ApiError::NotFound(_)) => "missing resource".to_string(),
            _ => "internal error".to_string(),
        },
    }
}

fn classify_error(e: &anyhow::Error) -> ErrorCode {
    match e.downcast_ref::<ApiError>() {
        Some(ApiError::Timeout) => ErrorCode::Unavailable,
        Some(ApiError::RateLimited) => ErrorCode::RateLimited,
        _ => ErrorCode::Unknown,
    }
}

struct Response {
    body: String,
}

enum ErrorCode {
    Unavailable,
    RateLimited,
    Unknown,
}
